w20: Compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, powered by AMD EPYC processors for balanced performance across diverse enterprise workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 provides 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, utilizing AMD EPYC 7B13 processors for balanced compute and memory workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors. It is designed for balanced performance across diverse enterprise tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor architecture. This configuration provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a 2.0 thread-per-core ratio, supported by 128 GB of RAM. The system includes 512 KB L1, 8 MB L2, and 128 MB L3 cache. It features dedicated CPU allocation and supports instruction sets including AVX, AES, and FMA, though it lacks GPU acceleration and local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and image processing capabilities, alongside top-tier memory performance and efficient small-model text generation. With an on-demand price of 0.9588 USD per hour, this instance provides a balanced profile for general-purpose computing. It is suitable for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and various data processing tasks where consistent multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are required for operational efficiency.
